name: onnozelaer-claude-marketplace
description: Personally created Claude Code plugins by Onnozelaer.

plugins.name: cabinet-of-imd
plugins.description: The Cabinet of IMD Agents — a flavour layer for Claude Code. Eight college classmates with distinct personalities, voices, disciplines, and pairings serve as specialised web-development agents. v3.0.0 sunsets all functionality (vault writes, session anchors, gate enforcement, state hooks) and keeps only character, voice, and working disciplines. Persistence is delegated to the adjudant plugin when active.

plugins.name: iteration-shelf
plugins.description: Terminal-aesthetic review boards for in-browser design iteration. Generates a curated shelf and a monster index from a JSON manifest — self-contained HTML, zero build step, on-demand iframe loading, sidebar outliner, and a warn-gate that keeps the browser from stalling on 100+ iterations. Explicit invocation only. Plugs into standalone design skills when installed; persistence via the adjudant plugin, with optional Cabinet voice flavour.

plugins.name: cli-wrapper-helper
plugins.description: The shared operating language for agent-built helper CLIs — one visual + interaction + safety language across interactive bash TUIs and python helper scripts, so every tool an agent builds looks, feels, and behaves the same.

plugins.name: gemineye
plugins.description: A review-only, sandboxed second opinion from a Gemini-family model via the Antigravity CLI (agy). Eight subcommands (review, megareview, wip, sanity, name, compare, save, harvest) with pinned Gemini models so the answer never silently comes from another model family; the reviewer is read-trusted to the project root, write-sandboxed, and proposes edits as code blocks that Claude applies. Pairs with adjudant for vault context and persisted reviews.

plugins.name: adjudant
plugins.description: Obsidian vault editor/writer and project initializer. One command, /adjudant, with eleven verbs — connect, port, sync, check, sitrep, tidy, ramasse, dream, draw, board, shelf — each backed by a stdlib-only Python helper that follows the .claude/adjudant breadcrumb to the vault (cross-machine via vault_name fallback and OB_VAULT). Locked three-tier cleanup model: tidy (surface mechanical, routine), ramasse (deep structural, sparing), dream (semantic content/knowledge refresh, judgment-heavy). draw creates canvases, bases, and mermaid diagrams — hand-authored or generated from vault data; board scaffolds a self-hosted drag-to-move kanban seeded from tasks/; shelf manages the six-state project lifecycle across vault zones; the board is ambient, born on the first task note and passively reseeded by hooks. Nine vault-aware hook entries across eight events keep session notes, a freshness-headed handoff, the kanban deck, and vault release records current; vault standards and output shape are schema-enforced and drift-guarded by 27 validators. Pairs with hookify and Gemineye; i-have-adhd is a soft dependency for output shape.
